localizations.title: The Secrets of The Octopus
localizations.hint_1: You have seen this portrait of The Octopus before... Maybe something before your adventure started.
localizations.hint_4: Should you feel the need to steal something, you can count on Baron Saturday. He is an expert in the field!
localizations.hint_6: It's a pretty flower, but you don't know much about plants. Keep sailing the seas, and perhaps you may come across someone more knowledgeable in such things.
localizations.hint_7: You don't have a penny on you, but the coin purse the soldier wears around his waist hasn't escaped your notice. Find a way to steal it.
localizations.hint_8: Your monkey won't be able to resist these for long.
localizations.hint_11: Ah, your monkey is stubborn, but you know him well. Find him a snack, and he'll be back in a flash.
localizations.hint_17_first: You must find a way to communicate with this ship. Keep exploring your surroundings to find more information about the East India Company.
localizations.hint_17: The lights on this ship are flashing. It reminds you of some kind of CODE. If you haven't done so yet, use your spyglass (80) and sail to the small deserted island. There you will find an oil lamp (73) and a message (13) mentioning the EDOC of the East India Company.
localizations.hint_19: With the soldier gone, you don't need to pay the docking tax, but you could still use this money to do business when the time comes.
localizations.hint_20: If you have no money, you must find (or steal) some.
localizations.hint_23: You need a rowboat. Haven't you seen one at the back of a ship?
localizations.hint_27: You must bring this concoction somewhere as close as possible to The Octopus. Where did he decide to end his life?
localizations.hint_31: The contents of this parchment seem to be a confession of The Octopus' sins and the suffering he inflicted on those around him; namely, he took The Shark's life, and ended his own. However, there are other sins he committed, and you must discover them.
localizations.hint_38: Focus on the ribbon of red light.
localizations.hint_39: With this rowboat, you can now enter areas that were unaccessible to your larger ship.
localizations.hint_50: She is a florist. If needed, you can talk to her for advice about plants.
localizations.hint_55:
These sins are all you have left; this is referencing the 3 remaining cards that haven't been revealed.
Reveal cards 75, 85, and 95. Each of these skulls (plus the one in the coffin) represents one of The Octopus' four sins. Once you have put the story in the proper order, they will give you the combination to the chest.
localizations.hint_56: You have found the flower on the deserted island (70). Bring the florist (56) to that island and position her card correctly in front of the plant of interest.
localizations.hint_62_first: What do you know about the voodoo island?
localizations.hint_62: The castaway's message (13) refers to this island. Its name is not entirely inconsequential.
localizations.hint_66_first: The curse on the chest (99) has been broken. You need to manipulate the 3 tentacles to orient them correctly. The 3 tentacles on the chest refer to 3 objects linked to the pirate "The Octopus."
localizations.hint_66: You encountered the first object at sea. The other two relate to the momentos the Voodoo Priestess kept of The Octopus.
localizations.hint_70: A man seems to have been marooned on this island, which is a haven for parrots.
localizations.hint_73_first: This oil lamp may prove useful in communicating with the East India Company ship. You still need to know the correct signals to send.
localizations.hint_73: The lights on the ship are flashing. It reminds you of some kind of CODE. The castaway's message (13) mentions the EDOC of the East India Company. That probably has something to do with it.
localizations.hint_75_first: Each of these skulls represents one of The Octopus' four sins. Once you have put the story in the proper order, the 4 skulls will give you the combination to the chest (99).
localizations.hint_75: Notice the golden tooth on the skull, as well as the insignia on its tricorn: a parrot. This skull refers to the marooning of the crew on Parrot Island (13).
localizations.hint_85_first: Each of these skulls represents one of The Octopus' four sins. Once you have put the story in the proper order, the 4 skulls will give you the combination to the chest (99).
localizations.hint_85: Notice the golden tooth on the skull, as well as the insignia on its tricorn: a snake. This skull refers to the broken heart of the Voodoo Priestess (99).
localizations.hint_90: The Voodoo Priestess has also suffered at the hand of The Octopus when he broke her heart before leaving her all alone here with the snake.
localizations.hint_95_first: Each of these skulls represents one of The Octopus' four sins. Once you have put the story in the proper order, the 4 skulls will give you the combination to the chest (99).
localizations.hint_95: Notice the golden tooth on the skull, as well as the insignia on its tricorn: a shark. This skull refers to the murder of Captain Rickon, AKA "The Skark" (31).
localizations.hint_99_first: The curse on the chest (99) has been broken. You need to manipulate the 3 tentacles to orient them correctly. The 3 tentacles on the chest refer to 3 objects linked to the pirate "The Octopus."
localizations.hint_99: You encountered the first object at sea. The other two relate to the momentos the Voodoo Priestess kept of The Octopus.
localizations.answer_hint_1: This portrait is the same as the one found on the game pack. One element in particular found in the portrait will help you open the chest (99).
localizations.answer_hint_6: Use the spyglass (80) and sail to Port Royal. There, you will meet a florist (50). Show her this flower (6).
localizations.answer_hint_7: Send your monkey (4) to steal the soldier's coin purse (7).
localizations.answer_hint_8: Once your monkey runs away, bring these peanuts (8) to Port Royal to lure him back.
localizations.answer_hint_11: When you have found the peanuts, bring them to town (11) to lure your monkey back.
localizations.answer_hint_17: The EDOC of the East India Company is its CODE in reverse. That CODE is generated by the lights of their galleon (80): dot, dash, dot, dot. Therefore, the EDOC is dot, dot, dash, dot. Use your oil lamp (73) to communicate with the East India Company (17).
localizations.answer_hint_19: After you meet with the captain of the East India Company galleon, give him this coin purse (19) to buy his extra rowboat.
localizations.answer_hint_20: After you have stolen the Port Royal soldier's coin purse, give it to the captain of the East India Company galleon (20) to buy his extra rowboat.
localizations.answer_hint_27: According to his final writings (31), The Octopus was in Port Royal when he took his life. Bring the concoction (27) to Port Royal (11).
localizations.answer_hint_38: The ribbon of red light escaping from the voodoo concoction forms two "5"s one after the other.
localizations.answer_hint_39: If you haven't done so yet, use your spyglass (80) and sail to the voodoo island, which is surrounded by dangerous reefs (23). Once there, maneuver your rowboat between the rocks to reach the island.
localizations.answer_hint_50: Use your spyglass (80) and sail to the deserted island. Once there, you will find a pretty yellow flower (6). Bring it to the florist (50).
localizations.answer_hint_55: Throughout the course of his adventurous life, The Octopus committed 4 sins. First, he killed John Rickon, AKA "The Shark" (31 and 95); then he marooned the crew on Parrot Island (13 and 75); next he met the Voodoo Priestess with the snake and broke her heart (90 and 85); and finally, he ended his own life in Port Royal (31 and 55). The gold teeth combination you must enter in the chest (99) is Shark, Parrot, Snake, Octopus.
localizations.answer_hint_56: Position the florist's card (50) on top of the deserted island (70) so that the pattern of flowers on her cape overlaps the flowers on the island. The small yellow flowers now form an 8.
localizations.answer_hint_62: NEWSENSE Island. The name of that island indicates the directions to follow: north, east, west, south, east, north, south, east.
localizations.answer_hint_66: The prow of The Octopus' ship (80), the Voodoo Priestess' necklace (66), and The Octopus' pipe (game pack). Orient the 3 tentacles of the chest (99) in the same way to open it.
localizations.answer_hint_73: The EDOC of the East India Company is its CODE in reverse. That CODE is generated by the galleon's flashing lights (80): dot, dash, dot, dot. Therefore, the ECOD is dot, dot, dash, dot. Use your oil lamp (73) to communicate with the East India Company ship (17).
localizations.answer_hint_75: Throughout the course of his adventurous life, The Octopus committed 4 sins. First, he killed John Rickon, AKA "The Shark" (31 and 95); then he marooned the crew on Parrot Island (13 and 75); next he met the Voodoo Priestess with the snake and broke her heart (90 and 85); and finally, he ended his own life in Port Royal (31 and 55). The gold teeth combination you must enter in the chest (99) is Shark, Parrot, Snake, Octopus.
localizations.answer_hint_85: Throughout the course of his adventurous life, The Octopus committed 4 sins. First, he killed John Rickon, AKA "The Shark" (31 and 95); then he marooned the crew on Parrot Island (13 and 75); next he met the Voodoo Priestess with the snake and broke her heart (90 and 85); and finally, he ended his own life in Port Royal (31 and 55). The gold teeth combination you must enter in the chest (99) is Shark, Parrot, Snake, Octopus.
localizations.answer_hint_95: Throughout the course of his adventurous life, The Octopus committed 4 sins. First, he killed John Rickon, AKA "The Shark" (31 and 95); then he marooned the crew on Parrot Island (13 and 75); next he met the Voodoo Priestess with the snake and broke her heart (90 and 85); and finally, he ended his own life in Port Royal (31 and 55). The gold teeth combination you must enter in the chest (99) is Shark, Parrot, Snake, Octopus.
localizations.answer_hint_99: The prow of The Octopus' ship (80), the Voodoo Priestess' necklace (66), and The Octopus' pipe (game pack). Orient the 3 tentacles of the chest (99) in the same way to open it.
localizations.machine_80_voodoo: You set sail for this sinister island.
localizations.machine_80_tentacle: You try to approach the Tentacle, but the sea rages and pushes you back.
localizations.machine_80_EastIndiaCompany: Intrigued by these lights, you weigh anchor and set sail toward this large ship.
localizations.machine_80_desertIsland: With the wind in your sails, you travel to the outskirts of this small island.
localizations.hint_62_first_2: Why did you come all this way? What can the Voodoo Priestess do to help you in your quest?
localizations.hint_62_2: You are here to break the curse on a chest… but not just any chest!
localizations.answer_hint_62_2: You want to break the curse on a chest protected by an octopus and a purple voodoo mist. Press the chest, the octopus, and the purple butterfly.
localizations.hint_62_first_3: You have seen this swirling symbol somewhere before.
localizations.hint_62_3: This ink swirl is also found on the back of each of the cards. But take a good look at them; this specific pattern is found on the back of one specific card.
localizations.answer_hint_62_3: This ink swirl is in the opposite direction to that on the back of the cards. However, note that the pattern on the back of card 66 is different, and is the only one to swirl in the same direction as the ink.
localizations.machine_73_error: This is not the correct sequence.
localizations.machine_73_wrong_order: It is not that simple. There must be a trick in the CODE of the East India Company.
localizations.machine_99_early: You must first break the curse on the chest that is on the voodoo island.
localizations.machine_99_wrong_order: That didn't work. You may have to do it in a certain order.
localizations.machine_62_step1: You row through the winding trail of mangroves, following the directions you worked out. Finally, you reach an area endowed with a mysterious atmosphere…
localizations.machine_66_question: Why have you come here, strangers?
localizations.machine_66_end_1: Ah! This chest…  that damned pirate….
localizations.machine_66_end_2: … the one who broke my heart…
localizations.machine_66_end_3: Very well, I will help you.
localizations.machine_73: You now know the code of the East India Company:
localizations.machine_99_step2: The chest opens. You expect to find the treasure inside, but to your great dismay, you find only a skull. The Voodoo Priestess turns to face you…
localizations.machine_99_end:
A few clinking noises come from the skull, and the false bottom unlocks. Underneath it is a bed of gold coins on which many precious objects rest. The treasure is now yours; you are rich! You finally set sail again, taking your loot from The Octopus' chest, but realize that the greatest treasure you found was not the gold coins, but rather the accomplishment of uncovering the secrets of the Octopus.
CONGRATULATIONS!
